notes_for_consumers: Feeds environment prompts and footage queries. Pick season-correct content for the cell's retail-calendar moment; the Year-End demo pulls winter. Southwest winter is WARM-light winter, not cold; the brief's snow-footage reels do NOT apply to this lane. This is the lane where the GL-vs-SW Q4 customization reads loudest. Low-confidence sensory mood (light/palette adjectives) is creative reference; the high-confidence climate-and-landscape content is the operative layer.

winter.retail_moment: Big Finish (Dec), Black Friday (Nov), Start Something New (Jan)
winter.weather: mild and dry across most of the BC; Phoenix lows in 40s, daytime 60s-70s; Hill Country and Albuquerque colder with overnight freezes; Big Bend and panhandle see real cold but no sustained snow; RGV stays warm. Snow is the exception, confined to higher elevations (Flagstaff, Ruidoso, Sandia).
winter.light: sharp, clear, low-angle; sun stays bright; long shadows midday; blue sky most days
winter.landscape: desert vegetation (saguaro AZ, mesquite and live oak TX, juniper NM); stucco and adobe; wide roads; strip-anchored retail; mountains visible from most metros
winter.activity_rhythm: outdoor work and recreation resume rather than pause; hunting season TX (white-tail) and NM; mild enough for active outdoor truck use; holiday lights against bare-branch oaks and palms
winter.cgi_footage_implication: Winter cells are warm-light winter, not cold. The brief's snow footage reels do NOT apply to this lane. This is the lane where GL-vs-SW Q4 customization reads loudest.

spring.retail_moment: Declaration of Deals (Mar-Apr), 4x4 Day, Truck Month
spring.weather: warm and dry, climbing fast; Phoenix into 90s by May; Hill Country 80s with humid afternoons; NM cooler at altitude; wildflower bloom (bluebonnets TX in April); dust and wind events common
spring.light: strong sun, longer days, lengthening shadows; wildflower color reads against beige and red ground
spring.landscape: bloom layer over muted ground; mesquite leafing out; mountain snowpack visible at distance through April in NM and Flagstaff; construction season ramping
spring.activity_rhythm: outdoor weekend activity at widest range before summer heat enforces early-morning timing; rodeo season TX

summer.retail_moment: Make This The Summer Event, Summer of Jeep
summer.weather: defining season. Sustained extreme heat Phoenix (100s, often 110s); high humidity coastal TX; dry heat interior; monsoon season begins late June AZ and NM (afternoon thunderstorms, dust walls, flash floods); triple-digit days set rhythm
summer.light: hard, direct, brutal midday; long golden hour at sunset locals know how to use; sunset is an event, not a backdrop
summer.landscape: dust haze interior; carports replacing garages (built for shade); pool culture metros; lakes and rivers full (Travis, Powell, Mead); big sky
summer.activity_rhythm: day compresses around heat; early morning and after-sundown are when outdoor work and life happen; trucks parked in shade or carport midday; boating concentrated weekends, often overnight or pre-dawn departures

fall.weather: heat breaks gradually; October first comfortable month in Phoenix; Hill Country and East TX see real fall by November; NM aspen color in October (Santa Fe, Taos); hurricane season risk along TX coast through October
fall.light: clearer than summer, less haze; color where it exists concentrated in aspen and mesquite turn; light softens but stays directional and sharp
fall.landscape: football culture takes over the calendar; high school stadiums lit Friday, college Saturday, NFL Sunday; State Fair of Texas in Dallas in October
fall.activity_rhythm: outdoor life returns full intensity; hunting season opens (dove September, then deer); bonfires and porch culture return; truck use peaks (hunting, football tailgates, year-end jobsite ramp)
